persona_name: Marcus — Returning User with Multiple Trips

ux_issues.description: AI response text contained garbled output in Morocco trip switch response: 'Fill meand dunes? Give-me-all-the-stars desert?' — appears to be a generation artifact.
ux_issues.suggestion: May be a transient LLM generation issue; monitor for recurrence.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI called create_itinerary_items 3 times in a single turn for the Santorini planning message. The first call successfully created 9 items, but the AI then called it twice more with the same items (both returned 'already_exists' with 0 created). This resulted in a repetitive response with 3 paragraphs essentially saying the same thing.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Phase 2b: Planning mode message asking about 3 days in Santorini. The AI's final response was triple the expected length because it appended confirmations after each redundant tool call.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The multi-trip workflow is solid. Vague reference matching ('my Greece trip' to 'Greek Islands Hopping') worked flawlessly. Trip switching correctly identified Morocco without creating a new trip, and New Zealand correctly triggered create_trip instead of select_trip. The 3-day Santorini itinerary was excellent in quality with 9 well-structured items across sightseeing, meals, and activities. The only notable issues were a triple tool-call loop producing a repetitive response and a minor garbled text artifact in the Morocco switch response.
